reforms.description: The Omnibus Trade and Competitiveness Act includes provisions aimed at improving the commercialization of U.S. R&D efforts, focusing on the rapid transfer of technology to industry through regional centers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the transfer of technology to industry, enhancing productivity and innovation in small and medium-sized firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was passed and provisions for commercialization were enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This Act represents a significant structural change in U.S. trade and innovation policy, aiming to enhance the competitiveness of U.S. industries through improved commercialization of research.
reforms.source_quote: A significant proportion of the Trade Act (the Omnibus Trade and Competitiveness Act) addresses the issue of improving the commercialisation of U.S. R&D effort.
